content: node --test glob handling: when globs are passed WITHOUT shell expansion (e.g. spawnSync or quoted args), node --test does its own glob expansion using minimatch-compatible ** patterns since Node 20+. On Node 18, passing 'test/**/*.test.mjs' directly to node --test does NOT expand ** — only shell expansion works. In CI (ubuntu GitHub Actions), the shell IS bash which supports **, but only when globstar is set (shopt -s globstar). GitHub Actions default shell for 'run:' steps is bash with non-interactive profile, so globstar may NOT be enabled. The safe fix is to list globs explicitly or use node --test --test-timeout=... with shell expansion guaranteed via bash -c. The concrete finding: CI yaml uses 'node --test test/*.test.mjs' (missing vue-sfc subdir = 8 tests missed), npm script uses 'node --test test/*.test.mjs test/**/*.test.mjs' (shell expands ** correctly in interactive bash). When node --test receives ** without shell expansion on Node 18, it silently finds 0 matching files in subdirs.
